source_jobs.title: Clinical AI Implementation Lead
source_jobs.description:
Clinically AI is a healthcare technology company transforming how clinicians document care, complete audits, and manage administrative workflows through artificial intelligence. Our platform reduces administrative burden, improves operational efficiency, and helps behavioral health and healthcare organizations focus more on care delivery and less on documentation and compliance overhead. Built with a strong emphasis on clinical usability, regulatory alignment, and workflow integration, we operate in a fast-growing and rapidly evolving AI healthcare market where adoption, trust, and execution matter.

We are seeking a Clinical AI Implementation Lead to join our team in a hybrid role, working on-site in our San Diego office Monday through Thursday. In this role, you will own end-to-end implementation, adoption, and workflow enablement for enterprise behavioral health and healthcare customers using the Clinically AI platform.

This is not a traditional software implementation role focused only on deployment and go-live milestones. Success in this position is measured by sustained usage, behavioral adoption, and long-term workflow integration across clinical, compliance, audit, and operational teams. You will help organizations become AI-native by guiding clinicians, administrators, and leadership teams through the transition from cautious experimentation to confident daily usage. You will help customers operationalize AI in a way that becomes embedded into daily clinical and administrative workflows

This role combines project management, workflow consulting, AI configuration, stakeholder leadership, and organizational change management. You will serve as the primary strategic and operational partner for customers throughout implementation and adoption, helping them integrate AI into real clinical and administrative workflows in a way that is practical, compliant, and sustainable over time.

This is a highly cross-functional role requiring strong communication, systems thinking, empathy, and operational leadership. You must be comfortable working across executives, therapists, compliance leaders, IT stakeholders, and frontline staff while maintaining clarity, momentum, and trust throughout the customer journey.

KEY ATTRIBUTES TO SUCCEED

 - Adoption-Focused Mindset: You measure success by sustained usage, workflow integration, and customer outcomes rather than simply completing implementation milestones.

 - Strong Project and Program Management: You are able to manage complex, multi-stakeholder implementations across clinical, operational, compliance, and technical teams while maintaining alignment and execution momentum.

 - Empathy and Change Leadership: You are comfortable guiding clinicians, administrators, and operational teams through uncertainty or skepticism related to AI adoption and new workflows.

 - AI-Native Thinking: You actively use AI tools in your own work and understand prompting, structured outputs, workflow automation, and practical AI application in operational environments.

 - Clinical Workflow Fluency: You understand healthcare and behavioral health documentation workflows, compliance requirements, and how clinicians and audit teams operate in practice.

 - Communication and Stakeholder Alignment: You are able to communicate effectively with executive sponsors, therapists, compliance teams, IT leaders, and frontline users while adapting your communication style to different audiences.

 - Systems and Operational Thinking: You understand how implementation, training, adoption, support, workflow optimization, and customer success connect into a unified operational system.

 - Thriving in a Fast-Paced Environment: You operate effectively in a high-growth startup where priorities evolve quickly and speed of execution matters.

REQUIRED:

 - 5+ years of experience in healthcare technology implementation, consulting, or clinical operations leadership

 - Demonstrated experience owning enterprise-grade implemen

source_jobs.title: Territory Director
source_jobs.description:
Clinically AI is a rapidly scaling healthcare AI company transforming how behavioral health and healthcare organizations manage clinical documentation, chart auditing, compliance workflows, and operational efficiency through artificial intelligence.

Our platform helps providers reduce administrative burden, improve documentation quality, strengthen audit readiness, and create more time for patient care. We operate at the intersection of healthcare operations, AI workflow transformation, and real-world clinical execution in a market where trust, adoption, workflow integration, and measurable outcomes matter deeply.

We are seeking multiple Territory Directors to lead regional market expansion across the Midwest, Southwest, and Mid-Atlantic regions.

This is not a traditional territory sales role focused solely on quota attainment or transactional software sales. Territory Directors at Clinically AI are responsible for building regional market presence, developing strategic customer relationships, independently conducting sophisticated product demonstrations, and helping healthcare organizations operationalize AI inside complex clinical and compliance-driven environments.

Customers are not simply evaluating software. They are evaluating whether AI can safely and realistically integrate into highly operational healthcare environments where documentation quality, reimbursement integrity, clinician burnout, compliance exposure, audit defensibility, and workflow efficiency directly impact organizational sustainability.

As a result, this role is deeply consultative, operational, and workflow-oriented.

Territory Directors are expected to independently lead live platform demonstrations throughout the sales process. This is not a sales motion where technical demos are fully owned by solutions engineers or implementation teams. Territory Directors must become highly fluent in the Clinically AI platform, behavioral health workflows, AI-assisted documentation, compliance operations, and operational transformation use cases in order to confidently guide sophisticated customer conversations autonomously.

Our demos are highly contextual, operational, and workflow-driven in nature. Territory Directors must be comfortable independently leading end-to-end platform demonstrations that connect AI capabilities directly to real-world clinical, compliance, and operational challenges within behavioral health and healthcare organizations. This includes navigating conversations around AI-assisted clinical documentation, ambient listening workflows, chart auditing and compliance operations, behavioral health documentation standards, EHR workflows and form structures, operational efficiency initiatives, reimbursement and compliance risk reduction, and broader organizational AI adoption concerns.

Successful Territory Directors are expected to operate independently throughout the sales process, including leading operational discovery conversations, conducting customized live demos, and translating complex AI functionality into practical workflow outcomes that resonate across multiple stakeholder groups. You must be comfortable dynamically tailoring demonstrations based on each organization’s workflows, documentation processes, operational pain points, and organizational structure while communicating effectively with executives, compliance leaders, operational stakeholders, IT teams, and frontline clinicians alike.

This role requires the ability to explain complex AI concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences while positioning Clinically AI not simply as a software vendor, but as a long-term operational transformation partner helping organizations improve documentation quality, strengthen compliance readiness, reduce administrative burden, and create more time for patient care.

Each territory operates with a high degree of ownership and autonomy. We view territory development as a long-term market-building initiative rather than a

source_jobs.title: Customer Support Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Clinically AI is a rapidly scaling healthcare AI company transforming how behavioral health and healthcare organizations manage clinical documentation, compliance workflows, chart auditing, and operational efficiency through artificial intelligence. Our platform allows clinicians, compliance teams, administrators, and healthcare organizations to reduce administrative burden, improve documentation quality, strengthen audit readiness, and operate more efficiently in increasingly complex healthcare environments. We operate at the intersection of AI, healthcare operations, workflow design, and real-world clinical execution in a market where adoption, trust, usability, and operational outcomes matter deeply.

We are seeking a Customer Support Specialist to deliver an exceptional customer experience while serving as a critical bridge between our customers, implementation teams, client success teams, and product organization. This is not a traditional call-center role focused solely on answering tickets and resolving basic issues. This is a highly visible, customer-facing position responsible for helping customers succeed, troubleshooting complex workflow and technical challenges, and ensuring that feedback from the field translates into meaningful operational and product improvements.

You will work at the center of customer experience, technical troubleshooting, workflow optimization, and cross-functional collaboration. You will engage with everyone from clinicians and administrators to executive leadership teams, helping customers navigate challenges while ensuring issues are resolved efficiently and professionally. This role requires balancing empathy with execution. You must be comfortable moving between customer conversations, technical troubleshooting, workflow discussions, issue escalation, stakeholder communication, and rapid problem-solving in a fast-moving startup environment. We are looking for a support professional who understands that exceptional support is measured not only by response times and ticket resolution, but by customer trust, platform adoption, operational outcomes, and the confidence customers have in our team and technology.

This is a unique opportunity to join a rapidly growing AI company at an early stage and have a direct impact on how customer support evolves as the organization scales. Unlike traditional support environments where roles can become narrowly focused, this position offers meaningful exposure to customers, healthcare operations, AI-powered workflows, product development, and executive decision-making. You will have the opportunity to build deep expertise in one of the fastest-growing segments of healthcare technology while working alongside a highly collaborative team that values ownership, initiative, continuous learning, and professional growth.

Success in this role is measured by the trust and confidence customers place in Clinically AI. The right candidate will help create an experience where customers feel heard, supported, and empowered to succeed on our platform. You will play a key role in ensuring issues are resolved thoughtfully and efficiently, feedback reaches the right internal teams, and support operations scale without sacrificing quality. As a result, customers, leadership teams, and internal stakeholders will view our support organization as responsive, professional, reliable, and deeply invested in customer outcomes.




KEY ATTRIBUTES TO SUCCEED

 - Professional Communication & Presence. You communicate clearly, confidently, and professionally with a wide range of stakeholders, including clinicians, administrators, IT teams, operational leaders, and executive leadership. You know how to adjust your communication style based on your audience while maintaining clarity and credibility.

 - Customer Empathy & Relationship Building. You genuinely care about helping customers succeed. You can de-escalate challenging situations, build trust quickly, and guide conversations

source_jobs.title: Quality & Reliability Engineer (AI Systems & Workflow Assurance)
source_jobs.description:
Clinically AI is a healthcare AI company transforming how clinicians, administrators, compliance teams, and healthcare organizations manage documentation, chart auditing, and operational workflows through artificial intelligence.

We are seeking a Quality & Reliability Engineer to ensure the stability, correctness, and performance of AI-native healthcare workflows and operational systems across the Clinically AI platform.

This is not a traditional QA role focused only on manual testing or end-of-cycle validation. This role sits at the intersection of software reliability, workflow systems testing, AI behavior validation, and operational quality engineering in complex healthcare environments.

You will ensure that clinicians, administrators, compliance teams, and operational users can confidently rely on AI-powered systems in real-world healthcare workflows. This includes validating system correctness, identifying edge cases in workflow behavior, improving release confidence, and ensuring that AI-driven outputs behave reliably within operational constraints.

The ideal candidate thinks deeply about systems, workflows, edge cases, failure modes, and operational behavior, not just test cases. You should be excited by ensuring reliability in AI-native systems where outputs may be probabilistic, workflows are complex, and correctness directly impacts clinical operations.

This role will work closely with product, engineering, implementation, and leadership teams in a highly collaborative and fast-moving startup environment.

KEY ATTRIBUTES TO SUCCEED

 - Systems Reliability Thinking You think beyond individual features and care deeply about how systems behave across workflows, services, and real-world usage conditions.

 - AI-Native Testing Mindset You understand how AI changes software behavior, especially in non-deterministic systems where outputs require workflow-aware validation.

 - Workflow Awareness You understand how clinicians, administrators, and operational teams interact with software in real environments under pressure.

 - Strong Analytical Judgment You can isolate failures across frontend, backend, APIs, and distributed systems with clarity and precision.

 - Collaboration & Communication You communicate effectively across engineering, product, and implementation teams.

 - Startup Adaptability You are comfortable iterating quickly, working through ambiguity, and helping define structure in evolving systems.

REQUIRED

 - 3+ years of experience in QA engineering, software testing, or reliability engineering roles in SaaS, web applications, or platform environments

 - Experience building automated test suites using Playwright or similar frameworks (Cypress, Selenium, WebdriverIO, etc.)

 - Strong understanding of frontend behavior, browser debugging tools, and network inspection

 - Experience validating REST APIs, including authentication, permissions, and data integrity

 - Proficiency in JavaScript or TypeScript for test automation

 - Experience working within CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions preferred)

 - Strong ability to identify root causes and communicate technical findings clearly

 - Experience collaborating closely with engineering and product teams in agile environments

 - High ownership mindset and ability to operate in fast-paced, evolving systems

PREFERRED BUT NOT REQUIRED (Nice to Have)

 - Experience testing React applications, browser extensions, or real-time web systems

 - Exposure to mobile testing environments (iOS/Android)

 - Experience with performance or load testing tools (k6, JMeter, Locust)

 - Familiarity with cloud infrastructure and distributed systems

 - Experience working in regulated environments (HIPAA, SOC2, NIST)

 - Experience testing AI/LLM-powered or non-deterministic systems

This is a full-time hybrid role based in San Diego, CA, with onsite work required Monday through Thursday.

We offer competitive compensation, equity participation, healthcare coverage, and flexible

source_jobs.title: Talent Acquisition Partner
source_jobs.description:
Talent Acquisition Partner

 

Clinically AI is the fastest growing company in Behavioral Healthcare transforming how clinicians document care, complete audits, and manage administrative workflows through artificial intelligence. We operate in a fast-growing and rapidly evolving AI healthcare market where adoption, trust, and execution matter.

 

We are the fastest growing AI company in behavioral healthcare. Our customers include therapists and some of the largest behavioral healthcare organizations in the country, the providers serving the safety net of society. As we scale, we are intentionally architecting the team and culture that will carry this company through its next stage of growth.

 

We are seeking a Talent Partner to join our People team in a hybrid role, working on-site in our San Diego office four days per week. In this role, you will own how we attract, evaluate, and hire the people who will define Clinically AI for the next decade. You will architect our hiring and talent strategy in close coordination with leadership, run aggressive sourcing campaigns during periods of rapid hiring, and build the disciplined recruiting infrastructure that lets us scale without compromising on quality or culture.

 

This is not a transactional recruiting coordinator role. Success in this position is measured by our ability to fill critical roles quickly without ever compromising on cultural alignment, technical bar, or long-term fit. You will operate as a strategic partner to executives, a hands-on sourcer for hard-to-fill seats, and the steward of our employer brand in the behavioral healthcare AI market.

 

While recruiting is the center of this role, the Talent Partner sits inside our People function and is expected to flex into broader HR responsibilities when the season calls for it. We have a dedicated HR leader, and you will partner closely with them, but during high-volume hiring cycles, onboarding waves, performance review periods, compensation planning cycles, or other moments of People-team load, you will roll up your sleeves and help carry HR work across the finish line. The right candidate is energized by the breadth and understands that great recruiting and great people operations reinforce each other.

 

Clinically AI is a pillar in the San Diego startup community. We host hackathons, industry meetups, and curated events that build our visibility, deepen our local network, and keep an active talent pool flowing into the company. In this role, you will own employer brand on the ground in San Diego, partnering with leadership to design and host signature events, hackathons, and community gatherings that align with the roles we are hiring for and the people we want to attract. The Talent Partner at Clinically AI is not just sourcing candidates, they are building the gravitational pull that brings the best of San Diego's technical and operational talent into our orbit.

 

You will maintain a consistent and warm pipeline of qualified talent across technical, GTM, G&A, clinical, and implementation functions. You will partner with hiring managers to translate role needs into structured scorecards, run efficient and respectful candidate experiences, and build the inbound, outbound, referral, and agency channels we need to hire at the pace our growth demands.

 

This is a highly cross-functional role requiring strong judgment, organizational discipline, executive presence, and a deep commitment to building a company where great people want to work. You must be comfortable moving quickly without sacrificing rigor, holding hiring managers accountable to the bar, and representing Clinically AI authentically to the candidates and communities we recruit from.

 

Key Attributes to Succeed

 

 - Talent Architect: You think in systems, not requisitions. You design intake processes, scorecards, interview loops, and pipeline strategies that scale across functions and survive periods of intense hiring volume.

 

 - Fast a

source_jobs.title: UX/UI Designer (AI Workflow & Product Experience)
source_jobs.description:
Clinically AI is a healthcare AI company transforming how clinicians, administrators, compliance teams, and healthcare organizations manage documentation, chart auditing, and operational workflows through artificial intelligence.

We are seeking a UX/UI Designer (AI Workflow & Product Experience) to shape the next generation of AI-native healthcare workflows and operational user experiences.

This is not a traditional visual design role focused only on polished interfaces or static design systems. This role sits at the intersection of workflow design, human-computer interaction, AI usability, operational systems thinking, and healthcare workflow optimization.

You will help design experiences that allow clinicians, administrators, compliance teams, and operational users to confidently integrate AI into their daily work. This includes reducing cognitive load, improving workflow clarity, designing AI-assisted interactions, and helping users trust and operationalize AI systems inside real-world healthcare environments.

The ideal candidate thinks deeply about workflows, systems, usability, behavior, and operational reality, not just screens. You should be excited by designing AI-native experiences where prompting, structured outputs, human review, workflow orchestration, and operational trust all matter.

This role will work closely with product, engineering, implementation, and leadership teams in a highly collaborative and fast-moving startup environment.



KEY ATTRIBUTES TO SUCCEED:

 - Workflow-Centered Design Thinking You think beyond interfaces and care deeply about how users actually move through operational workflows.

 - AI-Native UX Thinking You understand how AI changes interaction patterns, usability expectations, trust models, and workflow behavior.

 - Systems Thinking You understand how workflows, users, data structures, AI outputs, and operational processes connect together.

 - User Empathy You care deeply about reducing friction and improving usability for real users operating under pressure.

 - Collaboration & Communication You communicate effectively across product, engineering, implementation, and leadership teams.

 - Startup Adaptability You are comfortable iterating quickly, working through ambiguity, and refining workflows in real time.



REQUIRED:

 - 4+ years of experience in UX, UI, product design, interaction design, or workflow design roles

 - Experience designing workflow-heavy or operational software products across web, mobile, or enterprise environments

 - Strong portfolio demonstrating high-quality shipped UI work and strong systems thinking

 - Experience collaborating closely with product and engineering teams in fast-moving environments

 - Familiarity with AI-assisted workflows, conversational UX, structured outputs, or AI-native interfaces



PREFERRED BUT NOT REQUIRED (Nice to Have):

 - Healthcare technology or clinical workflow experience

 - Experience designing enterprise SaaS or operational platforms

 - UX engineering or frontend implementation experience (HTML/CSS, design tokens, etc.)

 - Familiarity with clinician-facing workflows, compliance systems, or administrative healthcare operations

 - Motion design or micro-interaction experience (SVG, Lottie, Rive, etc.)



This is a full-time hybrid role based in San Diego, CA, with onsite work required Monday through Thursday.

We offer competitive compensation, healthcare coverage, and flexible time off. The base salary range for this role is $95,000–$150,000 annually, depending on experience, product design expertise, UI craft quality, systems thinking capability, demonstrated performance, and overall scope alignment. In certain cases, we may consider candidates outside of this range whose background in AI-native product design, enterprise SaaS design systems, or complex workflow-driven interface design strongly aligns with the needs of the role. This role may also include additional long-term incentive opportunities aligned wi

source_jobs.title: Head of Product
source_jobs.description:
Clinically AI is a rapidly scaling healthcare AI company transforming how behavioral health and healthcare organizations manage clinical documentation, compliance workflows, chart auditing, and operational efficiency through artificial intelligence.

Our platform helps clinicians, compliance teams, administrators, and healthcare organizations reduce administrative burden, improve documentation quality, strengthen audit readiness, and operate more efficiently in increasingly complex healthcare environments. We operate at the intersection of AI, healthcare operations, workflow design, and real-world clinical execution in a market where adoption, trust, usability, and operational outcomes matter deeply.

We are seeking a Head of Product to lead product strategy, product execution, and cross-functional product development at Clinically AI in a hybrid role based in San Diego, CA.

This is not a maintenance-oriented product leadership role focused solely on roadmap management or feature prioritization. This is a foundational leadership role responsible for helping define the future of AI-native healthcare operations and translating customer realities into scalable product systems.

You will work at the center of product strategy, customer feedback, implementation learnings, AI workflow design, operational systems thinking, and executive decision-making. You will partner closely with leadership, implementation, engineering, design, and go-to-market teams to ensure Clinically AI builds products that are operationally useful, clinically aligned, technically scalable, and deeply embedded into customer workflows.

This role requires balancing strategic thinking with operational execution. You must be comfortable moving between customer conversations, product architecture discussions, workflow design, prioritization tradeoffs, AI interaction patterns, and rapid execution cycles within a fast-moving startup environment.

We are looking for a product leader who understands that in healthcare AI, adoption and workflow integration matter more than feature volume. Success is measured by sustained usage, operational outcomes, customer trust, and the platform becoming deeply integrated into how organizations actually work.

KEY ATTRIBUTES TO SUCCEED

 - Product Systems Thinking You understand how workflows, users, compliance requirements, operational constraints, AI interactions, and product systems connect together into one unified experience.

 - AI-Native Product Leadership You actively use AI tools in your own workflow and think deeply about how AI changes user behavior, interfaces, operations, and software design.

 - Customer & Workflow Obsession You care deeply about how clinicians, compliance teams, administrators, and operational leaders actually work in practice.

 - Strong Prioritization & Judgment You can simplify complexity, make thoughtful tradeoffs, and focus teams around the highest-leverage opportunities.

 - Cross-Functional Leadership You communicate effectively across engineering, implementation, leadership, operations, design, and customer-facing teams.

 - Startup Execution Mentality You are comfortable operating in ambiguity, moving quickly, iterating rapidly, and helping create structure where systems are still evolving.

 - Adoption-Focused Product Thinking You understand that successful healthcare products must become operationally embedded into daily workflows, not simply purchased.

REQUIRED

 - 7+ years of experience in product management, product leadership, or platform strategy roles within SaaS, healthcare technology, AI products, or workflow software environments

 - Experience leading product strategy and execution for complex workflow-driven or operational software products

 - Strong understanding of AI product design, AI workflows, prompt systems, structured outputs, or AI-assisted operational systems

 - Experience partnering closely with engineering and design teams in high-growth or fast-moving environment

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager (AI Adoption & Customer Outcomes)
source_jobs.description:
Clinically AI is an AI company transforming how clinicians, administrators, compliance teams, and healthcare organizations manage documentation, chart auditing, and operational workflows through artificial intelligence. The platform reduces administrative burden, improves documentation quality, strengthens compliance readiness, and drives operational efficiency through AI-native workflow automation.

We operate at the intersection of healthcare operations, clinical workflows, artificial intelligence, and real-world adoption, where success depends on trust, sustained usage, measurable outcomes, and deep workflow integration.

We are seeking a Customer Success Manager to drive customer adoption, retention, expansion, and long-term customer outcomes across the Clinically AI platform. This is a strategic, customer-facing role that sits at the intersection of customer success, workflow adoption, implementation execution, AI enablement, and revenue growth.

If you're the right person for this role, you think in terms of workflows, systems, and measurable outcomes rather than accounts or activity alone. You bring experience in Customer Success, Account Management, Implementation, Consulting, or similar customer-facing roles within SaaS, healthcare technology, or workflow-driven software environments. You have a proven track record of driving adoption, retention, renewals, expansion, and customer outcomes while managing complex stakeholder relationships across customer organizations.

You are comfortable operating cross-functionally with Sales, Product, Implementation, Support, and Engineering teams and thrive in fast-paced, high-accountability environments where priorities evolve quickly. You actively use AI tools in your daily workflow to improve productivity, communication, analysis, and execution, and you understand how AI can be embedded into real-world operational workflows to create measurable value.

In this role, you will own customer adoption, retention, expansion, and long-term value realization across an assigned book of business. You will serve as the primary advocate for your customers while ensuring they achieve the specific goals and outcomes that drove their decision to partner with Clinically AI. You will guide customers from implementation through renewal and expansion, ensuring AI becomes embedded into daily operations and delivers measurable business impact.

This role is central to Clinically AI's "Be Used" strategy, helping healthcare organizations confidently adopt AI technology, build AI capability across their teams, and realize meaningful operational transformation. Success in this role is measured by customer adoption, retention, expansion revenue, customer health, Success Criteria achievement, and long-term customer outcomes.

KEY ATTRIBUTES TO SUCCEED

 - Customer Outcome Orientation You focus on measurable outcomes, value realization, and customer impact rather than activity metrics or relationship management alone.

 - Workflow-Centered Thinking You understand how healthcare organizations operate in real clinical and administrative environments and how AI must integrate into those workflows to create lasting value.

 - AI-Native Execution You actively use AI tools in your daily workflow to improve speed, effectiveness, communication, and operational execution.

 - Systems Thinking You understand how implementation, adoption, utilization, customer health, retention, and expansion connect into a unified customer lifecycle.

 - Communication & Influence You communicate effectively across clinicians, executives, administrators, IT stakeholders, compliance leaders, and internal teams while driving alignment and execution.

 - Customer Advocacy You naturally champion customer needs internally while balancing business objectives and ensuring customers receive exceptional outcomes.

 - Startup Adaptability You thrive in ambiguity, rapid iteration cycles, evolving priorities, and high-accountability environments

notes: Healthcare AI company with strong emphasis on clinical usability and regulatory alignment.
